message:
fix(messaging/redis): add max_bulk_string_len limit and cap array pre-allocation against OOM

RedisProtocolLimits gains max_bulk_string_len (default 512 MiB),
enforced in the RESP decoder before allocating a bulk string buffer.
This prevents a malicious or buggy peer from sending a multi-gigabyte
length prefix that causes OOM before any payload arrives.

Additionally, RESP array pre-allocation is now capped at
n.min(1024) elements regardless of the declared array length. The
remaining elements are pushed during parsing. This closes a second OOM
vector where a single *999999999 line could allocate a massive Vec
before any elements are received.

New tests verify rejection of oversized arrays and bulk strings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
